title: Buttery Velvety Indian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A lazy Sunday Polish dish featuring chicken stock cube, bhuna spices for a rich and velvety texture, served as a side dish or light meal.

ingredients.name: bhuna spices blend (optional)
ingredients.notes: includes turmeric, cayenne pepper, red chili flakes

instructions: Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add onion, finely chopped garlic, and ginger. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until softened.
Add chicken to the pan and cook until golden brown on all sides (about 5-7 minutes).
Stir in coriander seeds, cumin seeds, and bhuna spices blend. Cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in chicken stock cube dissolved in a little water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
Simmer gently for about 20 minutes or until the sauce has thickened slightly (adjust seasoning if needed).
Serve hot with steamed basmati rice or naan bread.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
